result:
Both files land as specified: `<area>.onnx` (int8 FC, what the scorer reads) and `<area>_fp32_debug.onnx` (forensics copy). Implementation matches the pre-registered experiment exactly.

Summary of changes (all under `model/`):
- **`model/pretrained/mnv3s_features10.pt`** — new pretrained snapshot, `features[:11]` state dict (existing `mnv3s_features8.pt` left untouched).
- **`model/model.py`** — `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH` → features10; `_build_pretrained_trunk()` now loads `features[:11]` and applies the block-9 stride and block-10 dilation surgery after loading; `export_onnx()` exports fp32 to a sibling `_fp32_debug.onnx` then int8-quantizes only MatMul/Gemm into the final artifact; added an exp-26 docstring paragraph.
- **`model/train.py`** — only the `info["init"]` log string updated to `features[0..10]`.

Smoke test confirms exact predicted numbers: 1,305,002 total params (576,464 trunk), trunk output `(1,96,8,8)`, exported ONNX 3,091,446 bytes (≤4 MiB gate), ~4.6 ms single-thread latency (≤250 ms gate), valid `(u,v,conf)` output range. Ready for the harness to train and score.
